title: Base targets June 25 mainnet launch for Beryl upgrade and new B20 token standard
summary: Base's Beryl upgrade introduces the B20 native token standard, cuts withdrawal delays to 5 days, and activates on mainnet June 25.
body:
Base, the Ethereum Layer 2 network, is targeting a June 25, 2026 mainnet launch for its Beryl upgrade. The upgrade will introduce the B20 native token standard and reduce withdrawal delays to just 5 days, according to a statement from the team.

The Beryl upgrade is designed to improve token creation and management on the Base network. The new B20 token standard will allow developers to create and manage tokens more easily, potentially leading to a surge in new decentralized applications (dApps) and projects on the platform. The reduction in withdrawal delays will also make the network more user-friendly, as users will be able to access their funds more quickly.

For everyday users, the Beryl upgrade means a more efficient and user-friendly experience on the Base network. Faster withdrawals and a more robust token standard could attract more developers and users to the platform, potentially leading to a more vibrant and active ecosystem. This could also result in more opportunities for users to participate in various dApps and projects on the Base network.

One of the most notable aspects of the Beryl upgrade is the introduction of the B20 token standard. This new standard is expected to simplify the process of creating and managing tokens, making it easier for developers to build and deploy new projects on the Base network. The upgrade also includes several other improvements, such as enhanced security measures and better scalability, which will help ensure the long-term success of the network.

Users and developers should keep an eye on the June 25 launch date. Once the upgrade is live, they can expect to see faster withdrawals and a more streamlined process for creating and managing tokens. This could open up new opportunities for innovation and growth on the Base network, making it an exciting time for the crypto community.
twitterThread: Base is targeting June 25 for its Beryl upgrade, which will cut withdrawal delays to just 5 days and introduce the new B20 token standard.
The B20 token standard aims to simplify token creation and management on Base, while faster withdrawals mean quicker access to your funds.

If you're a developer or user on Base, mark June 25 on your calendar. The Beryl upgrade could open up new opportunities. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-19-base-to-launch-beryl-upgrade-and-b20-token-standard-on-june-25
